tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting possible chronic low glucose or malnutrition. Elevated creatinine may indicate impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, raising concern for renal impairment and possible dehydration. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation for renal and metabolic status.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
